# Chiel's Take

> Opinion column from Chiel Hendriks (Managing Director, PwC Canada; ex-Google, 16 years). Where the daily Ambient Advantage briefing reports what happened, this column tells you what it means: strategic perspective on the agentic and generative AI questions business leaders should be asking. Hand-written, no automation. For executives, consultants, and digital transformation leaders. Articles are short-form (4–5 minute reads) and updated irregularly when there's something worth saying.

## Data feeds
- [articles.json](https://take.ambient-advantage.ai/articles.json): Machine-readable index of every published take — slug, title, date, read time, excerpt. Sorted newest-first.
- [llms-full.txt](https://take.ambient-advantage.ai/llms-full.txt): Every published take inlined as full markdown for LLM consumption.

## Published takes
- [The AI users who are actually winning don't prototype. They ship.](https://take.ambient-advantage.ai/the-ai-users-who-are-actually-winning-dont-prototype-they-ship.html) (April 26, 2026): Cat Wu, Anthropic's Head of PM, on Lenny's Podcast — three insights on why most people aren't getting real value from AI yet, and what the ones who are doing differently.
- [CFOs Are Starting to Code (and SaaS Companies Should Pay Attention)](https://take.ambient-advantage.ai/cfos-are-starting-to-code.html) (April 20, 2026): A finance exec vibe-coded his way to a credible replacement for parts of his Workday and Concur stack. The SaaS moats that held for a decade are narrowing, and vendors coasting on comfort are more exposed than they realize.
- [Don't Flinch](https://take.ambient-advantage.ai/dont-flinch.html) (April 19, 2026): Organizations that abandon agentic AI projects at the first stumble are making a costly mistake. The gap between 85% and 97% accuracy is a prompting problem, not a model problem — and the companies that push through are building real advantages.
